subject: Update Spring 3PP versions
commit_message:
Update Spring 3PP versions

To address Security Issue CVE-2022-22965
https://spring.io/blog/2022/03/31/spring-framework-rce-early-announcement

Change-Id: Id719b9dc33d774b0d6516cc63ab7830d45deb27a
Signed-off-by: JohnKeeney <john.keeney@est.tech>
Issue-ID: CCSDK-3629

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Patch Set 1:

I would recommond using https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/spring-boot-setup/src/main/resources/pom-template.xml;hb=HEAD to generate springboot25 and springboot26 both. see if you can refactor same !

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I would recommond using https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/spring-boot-setup/src/main/resources/pom-template.xml;hb=HEAD to generate springboot25 and springboot26 both. see if you can refactor same !

Hi Kapil
I spent many hours trying to get that to template to work. 
I don't think the 25 version uses that template approach either. 
In the end it was Much easier to just re-write a new POM.
The suggestion from Dan Timoney was just to copy the 25 version and edit it. 

BTW, why have we so many parent POMs?   (1, 2, 25 & now 26)
Doesn't this somewhat defeat the idea of Parent POMs?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > I would recommond using https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=ccsdk/parent.git;a=blob;f=springboot/spring-boot-setup/src/main/resources/pom-template.xml;hb=HEAD to generate springboot25 and springboot26 both. see if you can refactor same !
> 
> Hi Kapil
> I spent many hours trying to get that to template to work. 
> I don't think the 25 version uses that template approach either. 
> In the end it was Much easier to just re-write a new POM.
> The suggestion from Dan Timoney was just to copy the 25 version and edit it. 
> 
> BTW, why have we so many parent POMs?   (1, 2, 25 & now 26)
> Doesn't this somewhat defeat the idea of Parent POMs?

I added to one of review I opened to let poms generated from template :
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ccsdk/parent/+/129038

We have multiple spring parents just to be flexible on choosing spring version.
There are apps still running with older spring versions but latest other 3rd party libs..
Yes, it's kind of defeating the purpose of Parent POM's, but it is what it is :)
Change has been successfully merged by Dan Timoney
